info.name: genCAT Integration Test Suite
info.description:
Postman test suite for the genCAT Archive & Records Management system.

Organized into six folders:
1. Auth Tests - Validates good/bad login credentials, identity verification, and logout.
2. Location Tests - Validates GET and POST location endpoints.
3. Box Tests - Validates GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE box endpoints.
4. File Tests - Validates GET, POST, PUT, DELETE file endpoints, search with filters, detail view, and status changes.
5. Loan Tests - Validates GET, POST, PUT, DELETE loan endpoints, checkout/return workflows, and queries by patron/staff.
6. Integration Test Flow - 12-step end-to-end workflow.

Pre-requisite: The Flask server must be running at {{baseUrl}} (default http://localhost:8080).

item.name: Auth Tests
item.description: Authentication test suite covering valid login, invalid login (bad username, bad password, empty credentials, missing fields), identity verification, and logout.

request.description: Seeds the database so user accounts are available for auth tests.

exec: pm.test('JWT cookie is set in response', function () {
// Save token for subsequent auth tests

request.description: Tests successful login with valid staff credentials. Expects 200 with an access_token in the response and JWT cookies set.
Tests successful login with valid patron credentials. Expects 200 with an access_token.
Tests login with a valid username but incorrect password. Expects 401.
Tests login with a non-existent username. Expects 401.
Tests login with empty username and password fields. Expects a non-200 error response.

exec: pm.test('Status code is not 200 (error response)', function () {
// Response might not be valid JSON, so check raw text

request.description: Tests login with no JSON body at all. Expects a 400 or 500 error.

item.name: Login - Re-authenticate for identity and logout tests

request.description: Re-authenticates with valid credentials to set the JWT token for the identify and logout tests that follow.

exec: pm.test('Response confirms the admin user', function () {

request.description: Verifies the identity of the currently authenticated user via the JWT token. Expects 200 with username and id in the response.

exec: pm.test('Response does not contain user identity', function () {

request.description: Attempts to access the identify endpoint without a JWT token. Expects 401 Unauthorized.

exec: // When unsetting cookies, the server sets them with empty/expired values

request.description: Logs out the current user by unsetting JWT cookies. Expects 200 with a 'Logged Out!' message.

item.name: Logout - Protected route rejected after logout

exec: pm.test('Status code is 401 (Unauthorized) after logout', function () {
pm.test('Cannot access identity after logout', function () {

request.description: After logging out, attempts to access a protected endpoint using the cleared token. Expects 401 Unauthorized, confirming logout was effective.

// This is validated by the assertion itself - a 400 means nothing was created

request.description: Attempts to create a location without the required geoLocation field. Expects 400.

Attempts to create a location with an empty geoLocation string. Expects 400.
Attempts to create a location where geoLocation is only whitespace. The endpoint strips whitespace, so this should be treated as empty. Expects 400.
Attempts to create a location without a JWT token. Expects 401.

item.name: Loan Tests

request.description: Seeds DB with 5 loans (3 returned, 2 active), 11 files, 5 patrons, 3 staff.
Auth for loan tests.

pm.test('Mix of active and returned loans', function () {
var active = pm.response.json().filter(function (l) { return l.returnDate === null; });
var returned = pm.response.json().filter(function (l) { return l.returnDate !== null; });

No JWT. Expects 401.

item.name: Integration Test Flow
item.description: The original 12-step end-to-end integration test covering Login -> Locations -> Boxes -> File Detail -> Loan Issue -> Active Loans -> Loan Check-In -> File Status Verification.

request.description: Drops and recreates all tables, then populates the database with seed data (4 locations, 7 boxes, 11 files, 8 users, 3 staff, 5 patrons, 5 loans).

item.name: Step 1 - POST /login -> staffuser/staffpass -> confirm redirect to /location

exec: // Store the token for use in subsequent requests

request.description: Logs in as a staff user. Uses admin/adminpass (seeded staff account). The API login endpoint returns a JWT access token and sets auth cookies for subsequent requests. Equivalent to the HTML form POST /login which would redirect to /location.

item.name: Step 2 - GET /location -> confirm Welcome message and 2 location buttons

exec: pm.test('Response contains location data as an array', function () {
pm.test('At least 2 locations are returned', function () {
pm.test('Each location has required fields', function () {
pm.test('Seeded locations include expected names', function () {

request.description: Retrieves all locations. Verifies at least 2 locations are returned (seed creates 4). The HTML page GET /location renders location buttons; this API equivalent returns location JSON.

exec: pm.test('Boxes at location 1 returned successfully', function () {
pm.test('Response is an array of boxes', function () {
pm.test('Location 1 has exactly 3 boxes (seed data)', function () {
pm.test('All boxes belong to location 1 with expected fields', function () {

request.description: Selecting location 1 redirects to its boxes. Uses GET /api/locations/1/boxes to retrieve boxes at location 1. Seed data places 3 boxes (BOX-A-001, BOX-A-002, BOX-A-003) at location 1.

exec: pm.test('Response is an array of boxes', function () {
pm.test('At least 3 boxes are returned', function () {
pm.test('All 7 seeded boxes are present', function () {
pm.test('Each box has a fileCount field', function () {

request.description: Retrieves all boxes from the system. Seed data creates 7 boxes across 4 locations.

exec: pm.test('Response is a single box object', function () {

request.description: Simulates searching for BOX001. Uses GET /api/boxes/1 to retrieve a single box (barcode BOX-A-001) as the equivalent of a keyword search returning 1 result.

item.name: Step 6 - GET /boxes/1/detail -> confirm 2 files listed (file1, file2)

request.description: Retrieves box 1 detail. Verifies the box contains exactly 2 files (file1 and file2 from seed data). The API returns fileCount.

item.name: Step 7 - GET /files/1/detail -> confirm Issue File button visible (status: Available)

exec: pm.test('File 1 exists and has correct ID', function () {
pm.test('Issue File button should be visible (status Available, no active loan)', function () {
pm.test('File 1 is a Student file type', function () {

request.description: Retrieves file 1 detail as JSON. When status is Available the HTML template renders an Issue File button. This test verifies the file status is Available and loanID is null.

item.name: Step 8 - POST /loans (fileID=1, patronID=1) -> confirm redirect + success flash

exec: pm.test('Loan checkout successful with status 201', function () {
pm.test('Response contains a valid loanID', function () {
pm.test('Response confirms 1 file was checked out', function () {
// Save loanID for use in Step 11 (check-in)

request.description: Issues file 1 to patron 1 (carol) by creating a new loan via checkout. The loanID is saved as a collection variable for use in Step 11.

item.name: Step 9 - GET /files/1/detail -> confirm Check In button visible (status: On Loan)

exec: pm.test('File 1 status is now On Loan', function () {
pm.test('Check In button should be visible (file is On Loan with a loanID)', function () {
pm.test('File 1 loanID matches the loan created in Step 8', function () {
pm.test('File 1 still belongs to box 1', function () {

request.description: After the checkout in Step 8, file 1 status should now be On Loan. When status is On Loan the HTML template renders a Check In button instead of Issue File.

item.name: Step 10 - GET /loaned -> confirm file1 appears in active loans list

exec: pm.test('Response is an array of active loans', function () {
pm.test('At least one active loan exists', function () {
pm.test('The loan from Step 8 appears in the active loans list', function () {
var found = jsonData.some(function (loan) { return loan.loanID === targetLoanID; });
pm.test('The loan from Step 8 has 1 file attached', function () {
var loan = jsonData.find(function (l) { return l.loanID === targetLoanID; });
pm.test('The loan has no return date (still active)', function () {
var loan = jsonData.find(function (l) { return l.loanID === targetLoanID; });
pm.test('The loan is assigned to patron 1', function () {
var loan = jsonData.find(function (l) { return l.loanID === targetLoanID; });

request.description: Retrieves all active (unreturned) loans. Verifies that the loan created in Step 8 appears in the active loans list. Equivalent to the HTML page GET /loaned.

exec: pm.test('Loan check-in successful with status 200', function () {
pm.test('Response contains the correct loanID', function () {
pm.test('Response contains a return date', function () {

request.description: Checks in (returns) the loan created in Step 8. Uses PUT /api/loans/{loanID}/return which sets all attached files back to Available. Equivalent to the HTML form POST /loans/{loanID}/checkin.

item.name: Step 12 - GET /files/1/detail -> confirm Issue File button back (status: Available)

exec: pm.test('File 1 status is back to Available after check-in', function () {
pm.test('Issue File button should be visible again (status is Available)', function () {
pm.test('File 1 still has correct attributes', function () {
pm.test('Full loan lifecycle complete: Available -> On Loan -> Available', function () {
console.log('Integration test complete: file 1 successfully cycled through loan and return.');

request.description: After checking in the loan in Step 11, file 1 status should be back to Available with loanID cleared. The HTML template renders the Issue File button again. This final step verifies the complete loan lifecycle: Available -> On Loan -> Available.
